sanskrit:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥
transliteration: prāpya puṇya-kṛtāṃ lokān uṣitvā śāśvatīḥ samāḥ | śucīnāṃ śrīmatāṃ gehe yoga-bhraṣṭo ’bhijāyate ||
literal_meaning: Having attained the worlds of the meritorious and dwelt there for many long years, the fallen yogī is born into a home of the pure and prosperous.
contextual_meaning: Even an incomplete inner journey is never wasted; the psyche preserves its gains, and future circumstances will re-supply the necessary supports for growth.
psychological_state: Post-setback optimism: the mind realizes that partial effort still accumulates psychological capital, reducing shame and re-igniting motivation.
trigger_scenario: You abandon a meditation app after three months, assume you’ve ‘failed,’ then receive a scholarship to a mindful-leadership program—your past practice quietly prepared you.
anchor_text: Every step toward the Self is banked; destiny returns you to the path.
